blocks.content: The 34 came and she didn't move.
I didn't point it out. She didn't say anything about it. We just kept talking and the bus came and went and neither of us acknowledged that.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: What's it like — being on the team? Like actually on it, not trying out.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Like I finally understand what I was trying out for. It was different before. I didn't know what I was asking to be part of.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Coach doesn't put freshmen in unless he has to.
She said it like she already believed it. Not the way people say things to be nice — the way people say things when they've decided.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I might come to a game. I've never actually been to one.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You've been here three years and never been to a game?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Nobody ever made it feel like something I should see.
I didn't know what to do with that. So I didn't do anything with it. I just let it sit there and it sat there fine.
She said it like she didn't mind at all.
